diagrams.title: Disk Structure and Request Queue
diagrams.description: Shows the physical structure of a magnetic disk with concentric tracks, sectors, and the disk arm/head. Also displays a sample queue of I/O requests with their track numbers waiting to be serviced.
elements: disk arm and read/write head indicator
diagrams.purpose: Establishes foundational understanding of disk anatomy and introduces the scheduling problem context before showing algorithm behaviors

diagrams.title: FCFS vs SSTF Head Movement Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing the disk arm's path when servicing the same set of requests using First-Come-First-Served and Shortest Seek Time First algorithms. Shows track numbers on horizontal axis and animated path lines.
elements: FCFS path line showing chronological order
SSTF path line showing closest-first order
color-coded path lines for each algorithm
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the fundamental difference between simple FCFS scheduling and optimized SSTF, visually showing how SSTF reduces total head movement

diagrams.title: SCAN and C-SCAN Algorithms Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows the characteristic back-and-forth (SCAN) and circular (C-SCAN) movement patterns of the disk head across tracks. Uses a simplified linear track representation to clearly show the sweeping motion.
elements:: SCAN algorithm: head moves right, reverses at end, sweeps back
C-SCAN algorithm: head moves right, jumps to beginning, continues
numbered service order for each algorithm
diagrams.purpose: Explains the sweep-based algorithms (SCAN family) which are more fair than SSTF, showing how they avoid starvation and provide uniform response time
